questions.question: What is heat?
questions.correct_answer: A form of energy that flows from a hotter object to a cooler one
questions.explanation: Heat always transfers from a hot body to a cold body until equilibrium is reached.

questions.question: What is temperature?
questions.correct_answer: A measure of how hot or cold an object is
questions.explanation: Temperature measures the intensity of heat energy in a body.

questions.question: What is conduction?
questions.correct_answer: Transfer of heat through a solid without movement of the material
questions.explanation: Heat flows from the hotter part to the cooler part through particle collisions.

questions.question: Give an example of conduction.
questions.correct_answer: A metal spoon gets hot when its other end is in hot soup
questions.explanation: The heat travels along the metal without the metal moving.

questions.question: What is convection?
questions.correct_answer: Transfer of heat by movement of fluid (liquid or gas)
questions.explanation: Hot fluid rises while cooler fluid sinks, forming convection currents.

questions.question: Give an example of convection.
questions.correct_answer: Boiling water circulates as hot water rises and cool water sinks
questions.explanation: This motion carries heat throughout the water.

questions.question: What is radiation?
questions.correct_answer: Transfer of heat in the form of electromagnetic waves without a medium
questions.explanation: Heat can travel through vacuum by radiation, like sunlight reaching Earth.

questions.question: What is a conductor of heat?
questions.correct_answer: A material that allows heat to pass through easily
questions.explanation: Metals are good conductors, transferring heat efficiently.

questions.question: What is an insulator of heat?
questions.correct_answer: A material that resists the flow of heat
questions.explanation: Wood, plastic, and air are good insulators.

questions.question: How does heat transfer affect weather?
questions.correct_answer: Heat transfer in atmosphere through radiation and convection drives winds and weather patterns
questions.explanation: Solar heating of Earth causes air movement and circulation.

questions.question: How do oceans help in heat transfer?
questions.correct_answer: Oceans absorb solar energy, creating convection currents that distribute heat globally
questions.explanation: This regulates climate and affects marine life.

questions.question: Give an example of heat transfer application in nature.
questions.correct_answer: Greenhouses trap heat by radiation to grow plants in cold climates
questions.explanation: Understanding heat transfer helps in designing human-made structures and using energy efficiently.

notes.title: Heat vs Temperature
notes.content: Heat = energy transfer; Temperature = measure of heat intensity.

notes.title: Conduction
notes.content: Heat transfer in solids through direct particle contact.

notes.title: Convection
notes.content: Heat transfer in liquids/gases through fluid movement.

notes.title: Radiation
notes.content: Heat transfer through electromagnetic waves, does not need a medium.

notes.title: Conductors and Insulators
notes.content: Conductors allow heat flow; insulators resist it.

facts.fact: Heat always flows from a hotter object to a cooler object.
Convection currents in oceans help regulate global climate.
Radiation allows the sun's heat to reach the Earth through space.
Insulators like wood and plastic are used to prevent unwanted heat transfer.
Understanding heat transfer helps in architecture, greenhouses, and renewable energy applications.
